mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and equity prices higher. The proxy RSAFS captures consumer demand directly, and its upward trend predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or a regression of 20-day forward returns on RSAFS monthly growth yields insignificant coefficient (p>0.10) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops decisively below the 5% threshold, it indicates a tightening labor market that supports consumer confidence and spending. This positive labor market signal leads to higher equity prices over the following month as investors price in sustained growth.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0 after being >= 5.0 in the prior month
failure_condition: In the 12 months following each trigger event, the forward 20-day return median is not significantly positive (Wilcoxon test p>0.05) or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently very low federal funds rates compress the risk-free rate and encourage borrowing and equity investment. The current level of 0.4% is extremely accommodative. This low-rate environment supports positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon as liquidity flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: Whenever the trigger is active, the average 5-day forward return is negative or not significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.10) over the full period since 2009.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of low inflation and low interest rates creates an ideal backdrop for equity valuation: stable prices allow the Fed to keep rates low, reducing discount rates on future cash flows. When both CPIAUCSL growth is moderate and FEDFUNDS is near zero, the combined effect amplifies positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL * FEDFUNDS) in a linear regression of 20-day forward returns fails to reach statistical significance (p>0.05) when controlling for individual series, over any 5-year window since 2000.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion when the labor market is near full employment (UNRATE <= 5%) and payroll growth is still positive (PAYEMS monthly change > 100k), the economy is peaking. This regime historically precedes a downturn, and forward 60-day equity returns tend to be negative as investors anticipate a recession.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE <= 5% and PAYEMS monthly change > 100k, the average 60-day forward return is positive or not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.10) over a 20-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity and supply-side strength. A threshold of consecutive monthly gains above 0.3% signals a robust production cycle that boosts earnings and equity prices over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 0.3% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: After each trigger event, the median 60-day forward return is not positive or the proportion of positive returns is less than 60% (binomial test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above the 1.2 million annualized unit threshold signal a healthy construction sector and strong residential investment. This boosts related industries and consumer wealth effects, leading to positive equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months when HOUST exceeds 1200, the average 20-day forward SPX return is below zero or not statistically different from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of employment growth and retail sales growth provides a confirmed signal of consumer-led demand. When both PAYEMS and RSAFS rise month over month, it indicates synchronized improvement in labor income and spending, strongly supporting corporate earnings and equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: The rank IC of the interaction signal (equal-weighted composite of PAYEMS and RSAFS month-over-month growth) over 5-day forward returns is below 0.05 in any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in at least 55% of occurrences.
